::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›

When a character reaches the 20th level of a class, she gains a powerful class feature or ability, sometimes referred to as a capstone. The following section provides new capstones for characters to select at 20th level. A character can select one of the following capstones in place of the capstone provided by her class. Some capstones are for specific classes, while others are for a range of classes that qualify for them. In some cases, a capstone specifies what ability it replaces. A character can't select a new capstone if she has previously traded away her class capstone via an archetype. Clerics and wizards can receive a capstone at 20th level, despite not having one to begin with.
| Capstone | Benefit |

| Adoring Fan (Su) | At 20th level, the bard can use his performance to convert even the toughest audience. To be affected, a target must be able to see and hear the bard perform for 1 full round and be within 30 feet. The target can attempt a Will save (DC = 10 + half the bard's level + the bard's Charisma modifier) to negate the effect. If a creature succeeds at the saving throw, the bard cannot use this ability on that creature again for 24 hours. On a failure, the creature is dominated (as ‹spell/dominate monster›) for as long as the bard keeps playing and is charmed (as ‹spell/charm monster›) for 1d4 hours thereafter. Adoring fan is a mind-affecting, compulsion effect that relies on audible and visual components. |

| Deep Magics (Su) | At 20th level, the character's repertoire of spells deepens dramatically. She gains an additional spell known for each spell level she can cast. A character of any class with spells known can select this capstone. |

| Perfect Body, Flawless Mind (Ex) | At 20th level, the character's endless training and study has resulted in an unmatched mastery of the self. The character increases her ability scores by a collective total of 8. For example, she can increase one score by 8, or one score by 5 and another by 3, or four scores by 2, and so on. Characters of any class can select this ability. |

| The Boss (Ex) | At 20th level, the character has become more than just a lone hero - she has become one of the senior figures of her field, with powers and responsibilities to match. The character becomes one of the leading figures in some manner of group or organization, as appropriate to the campaign and the setting. A wizard might become the dean of an arcane university or mages' guild, a fighter could command a mercenary army or a city guard, a cleric might lead a major temple or her own sect, and so forth. The player and the GM should work together to determine the specifics. The character gains the ‹feat/Leadership› feat if she does not already have it, and the number of followers that the feat grants is multiplied by 10 (although depending on the campaign and setting, the position may grant other powers as well). If multiple characters in a party select this capstone, the GM may consider pooling them to grant the players a particularly large and powerful organization, such as a small kingdom. Characters of any class can select this ability. |

| With This Sword (Ex) | At 20th level, the character's blade has become as well-known as the character herself. The character selects one item she has - preferably something iconic and significant, such as a weapon or arcane bond. The item becomes a ‹rule/minor »artifact<s› and gains 100,000 gp worth of new powers. The player and the GM should work together to select the new powers, with an eye towards making something memorable yet campaign-appropriate. Characters of any class can select this ability. |

| Won't Stay Dead (Ex) | At 20th level, the character becomes a paragon of resilience. Once per week, if the character is killed, petrified, or otherwise removed from play, the character manages to survive by some dint of skill or luck and returns at the end of the combat or the scene (GM's discretion). The player and the GM should work together to ensure that the method of the character's survival is at least vaguely plausible, if unlikely. Characters of any class can select this ability. |
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::blockquote{className="hanging striped"}
**Aasimar:** Choose one bardic performance; treat the bard as +1/6 level higher when determining the effects of that performance.[^A]
**Boggard:** Reduce the amount of time the bard must wait before using his terrifying croak ability by 5 minutes (minimum 5 minutes).[^B]
**Catfolk:** Add +1/2 to the bard's bardic knowledge bonus.[^C]
**Drow:** Add +1/2 to Bluff and Disguise checks to appear as an elf or half-elf.[^D]
**Duskwalker:** Add 1/2 to the bard's bardic Knowledge bonus.[^E]

**Dwarf:** Reduce arcane spell failure chance for casting bard spells when wearing medium armor by +1%. Once the total reaches 10%, the bard also receives Medium Armor Proficiency, if he does not already possess it.[^F]
**Elf:** Add +1 to the bard's CMD when resisting a disarm or sunder attempt.[^G] [^H]
**Fetchling:** Add a +1 bonus on Disguise checks when using ‹spell/disguise self›.[^I]
**Ganzi:** Add 1/2 to the bard's bardic knowledge bonus.[^J]
**Gathlain:** Add one spell from the druid spell list to the bard's spell list and known spells. This spell must be at least 1 level lower than the highest-level spell the bard can cast.[^K] [^L]
**Gillman:** Add 1/2 to the bard's Bluff checks to fool someone and gain a +1/2 bonus on the bard's Sense Motive checks to sense enchantments.[^M]
**Gnome:** Add +1 to the bard's total number of bardic performance rounds per day.[^N] [^O]
**Goblin:** Add +1 to the bard's total number of bardic performance rounds per day.[^P] [^Q]
**Grippli:** Add 5 feet to the range of one bardic performance.[^R]
**Half-elf:** Add +1 to the bard's total number of bardic performance rounds per day.[^S] [^T]
**Halfling:** Add +1/2 on Bluff checks to pass secret messages, +1/2 on Diplomacy checks to gather information, and +1/2 on Disguise checks to appear as an elven, half-elven, or human child.[^U] [^V]
**Half-orc:** Add +1 to the bard's total number of bardic performance rounds per day.[^W]
**Half-orc:** Gain a +1/2 bonus on Bluff checks to pass secret messages, Sense Motive checks to discern secret messages, and Linguistic checks to decipher unusual handwriting.[^X]
**Human:** Add one spell known from the bard spell list. This spell must be at least one level below the highest spell level the bard can cast.[^Y] [^Z]
**Ifrit:** Add +1/6 to the number of people the bard can affect with the fascinate bardic performance.[^1]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^2]
**Kitsune:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Bluff checks to lie and a +1/2 bonus on Diplomacy checks to gather information.[^3]
**Kobold:** Treat the bard's level as +1/2 level higher for the purpose of determining the effect of the fascinate bardic performance.[^4]
**Merfolk:** The bard learns 1/6 of a new bardic masterpiece. (The bard selects the masterpiece once he has made this selection six times and must meet its prerequisites when it is selected.)[^5]
**Oread:** Add +5 feet to the range of one of the bard's bardic performances (max +30 feet to any one performance).[^6]
**Ratfolk:** Increase the bonus provided by the bard's inspire competence performance by 1/6, and add 1/6 to the number of allies the bard can affect with his inspire greatness performance.[^7]
**Shabti:** Add 1/2 to the bard's bardic knowledge bonus.[^8]
**Tengu:** Gain a +1/3 bonus on Perform checks attempted as part of countersong and distraction bardic performances.[^9]
**Undine:** Add a +1 bonus on Perform checks to use the countersong bardic performance against creatures with the aquatic or water subtypes.[^0]
**Vanara:** Add 1/2 to the bard's bardic Knowledge bonus.[^!]
**Vine leshy:** Add a +1/3 bonus to Perform (oratory) checks, including checks using versatile performance, as long as the bard first spends at least 10 minutes telling a story.[^@]
**Vishkanya:** Add +1 to the bard's total number of bardic performance rounds per day.[^&]

**Wayang:** Add one spell known from the wizard's illusion school spell list. This spell must be at least one level below the highest spell level the bard can cast. The spell is treated as being one level higher, unless it is also on the bard spell list.[^%]

::archetype[Voice of the Wild]{c=bard r="Bardic Knowledge; Countersong; Versatile Performance; Jack-of-All-Trades; Inspire Competence; Dirge of Doom; Inspire Heroics" e="Most bards are inspired by the art of civilization, yet the voice of the wild's muse is the grandeur and beauty of nature."}

::archetype[Wasteland Chronicler]{c=bard r="Bardic Knowledge; Inspire Competence" e="Wasteland chroniclers explore the farthest reaches of desolate wastelands, seeking to unlock the mysteries found there and meet the inhabitants of such regions"}

::archetype[Watersinger]{c=bard r="Fascinate, Suggestion, Mass Suggestion, Inspire Competence, 5th-level Lore Master" e="(Undine Only) The watersinger's song reaches from the depths of his soul into the elemental waters from which life first sprang."}

::archetype[Wit]{c=bard r="Bardic Knowledge; Inspire Competence; Dirge of Doom; Frightening Tune; Versatile Performance; Lore Master; Jack-of-All-Trades" e="The wit is a master of clever repartee and verbal dueling, moving through noble courts like a fish through water."}

bloodrager.name: Bloodrager
description: ## Bloodrager
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/15›

While many ferocious combatants can tap into a deep reservoir of buried rage, bloodragers have an intrinsic power that seethes within. Like ‹class/sorcerer«s›, bloodragers' veins surge with arcane power. While sorcerers use this power for spellcasting, bloodragers enter an altered state in which their bloodline becomes manifest, where the echoes of their strange ancestry lash out with devastating power. In these states, bloodragers can cast some arcane spells instinctively. The bloodrager's magic is as fast, violent, and seemingly unstoppable as their physical prowess.

**Role:** Masters of the battlefield, bloodragers unleash fearful carnage on their enemies using their bloodlines and combat prowess. The bloodrager's place is on the front lines, right in his enemies' faces, supplying tremendous martial force bolstered by a trace of arcane magic.
::class{al=Any any hd=10 parent="‹class/Barbarian› and sorcerer" wealth=3 skp=4 acro climb craft ha intm ka per ride spc sur swim}
### Class Features and Spells Per Day
| Level | Base Attack Bonus | Fort Save | Ref Save | Will Save | Special | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th |
| 1st | +1 | +2 | +0 | +0 | Bloodline, bloodline power, bloodrage, fast movement | - | - | - | - |
| 3rd | +3 | +3 | +1 | +1 | Blood sanctuary | - | - | - | - |
| 4th | +4 | +4 | +1 | +1 | Blood casting, bloodline power, eschew materials | 1 | - | - | - |
| 5th | +5 | +4 | +1 | +1 | Improved uncanny dodge | 1 | - | - | - |
| 7th | +7/+2 | +5 | +2 | +2 | Bloodline spell, damage reduction 1/- | 1 | 1 | - | - |
| 10th | +10/+5 | +7 | +3 | +3 | Bloodline spell, damage reduction 2/- | 2 | 1 | 1 | - |
| 12th | +12/+7/+2 | +8 | +4 | +4 | Bloodline feat, bloodline power | 2 | 2 | 1 | - |
| 13th | +13/+8/+3 | +8 | +4 | +4 | Bloodline spell, damage reduction 3/- | 3 | 2 | 1 | 1 |
| 16th | +16/+11/+6/+1 | +10 | +5 | +5 | Bloodline spell, bloodline power, damage reduction 4/- | 3 | 3 | 2 | 1 |
| 20th | +20/+15/+10/+5 | +12 | +6 | +6 | Bloodline power, mighty bloodrage | 4 | 4 | 3 | 2 |
### Spells Known
| Level | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th |

::prof{simple martial light medium shields extra="You can cast bloodrager spells while wearing light armor or medium armor without incurring the normal arcane spell failure chance. This does not affect the arcane spell failure chance for arcane spells received from other classes. Like other arcane spellcasters, heavy armor or wielding a shield incurs a chance of arcane spell failure if the spell in question has somatic components."}

::ab[Bloodline (Basics)]{jl=Bloodline l=1 icon=magic-boost ability="You must pick one ‹ability/bloodrager_>bloodline<s› upon taking your first level of bloodrager. Once made, this choice cannot be changed. You immediately gain its 1st-level power." imp4="You gain the 4th-level bloodline power." imp6="You gain a bonus feat chosen from a list of feats presented in your bloodline." imp7="You learn an additional spell from your bloodline." imp8="You gain the 8th-level bloodline power." imp9="You gain a second feat from your bloodline." imp10="You learn an additional spell from your bloodline." imp12="You gain the 12th-level bloodline power and a third bloodline feat." imp13="You learn an additional spell from your bloodline." imp15="You gain a fourth bloodline feat." imp16="You gain the 16th-level bloodline power, and learn an additional spell from your bloodline." imp18="You gain a fifth bloodline feat." imp20="You gain the 20th-level bloodline power." special="For all spell-like bloodline powers, treat your bloodrager level as the caster level. Spells you gain from your bloodline are in addition to the number of spells given per level. These spells cannot be exchanged for different spells at higher levels."}

Each bloodrager has a source of magic somewhere in his heritage that empowers his bloodrages, bonus feats, and bonus spells. Sometimes this source reflects a distant blood relationship to a powerful being, or is due to an extreme event involving such a creature somewhere in his family's past. Regardless of the source, this influence manifests in a number of ways.

When choosing a bloodline, the bloodrager's alignment doesn't restrict his choices. A good bloodrager could come from an ‹blrgbloodline/abyssal› bloodline, a ‹blrgbloodline/celestial› bloodline could beget an evil bloodrager generations later, a bloodrager from an ‹blrgbloodline/infernal› bloodline could be chaotic, and so on. Though his bloodline empowers him, it doesn't dictate or limit his thoughts and behavior.

@HL[NOTE:] If the bloodrager takes levels in another class that grants a bloodline, the bloodlines must be the same type, even if that means that the bloodline of one of the classes must change. Subject to GM discretion, the bloodrager can change his former bloodline to make them conform.
:::aside
Optionally, whenever a bloodrager gains a new bloodline power or bonus feat, he can swap it for a ‹ability/bloodline mutation<s› whose prerequisites he meets.

::ab[Bloodrage (Su)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=power-boost flavor="Your source of internal power grants you the ability to bloodrage." free="You can enter or leave a bloodrage as a free action. While in a bloodrage, you gain a +4 morale bonus to your Strength and Constitution, as well as a +2 morale bonus on Will saves. In addition, you take a -2 penalty to Armor Class. The increase to Constitution grants you 2 hit points per Hit Die, but these disappear when the bloodrage ends and are not lost first like ‹rule/temporary hit points›. While bloodraging, you cannot use any Charisma-, Dexterity-, or Intelligence-based skills (except Acrobatics, Fly, Intimidate, and Ride) or any ability that requires patience or concentration." special="When the bloodrage ends, you are ‹misc/fatigued› for a number of rounds equal to twice the number of rounds spent in the bloodrage. You can't enter a new bloodrage while fatigued or ‹misc/exhausted›, but can otherwise enter bloodrage multiple times during a single encounter or combat. If you fall ‹misc/unconscious›, your bloodrage immediately ends, placing you in peril of death." usage="You can bloodrage for 2 + twice your level + your Con modifier rounds/day; these rounds need not be consecutive. Temporary increases to Con (such as those gained from bloodraging or spells like ‹spell/bear's endurance›) don't increase this number."}
@HL[NOTE:] Bloodrage counts as the ‹class/barbarian«'s› *rage* class feature for the purpose of feat prerequisites, feat abilities, magic item abilities, and spell effects.

::ab[Fast Movement (Ex)]{jl=strip icon=boost l=1 passive="Your land speed is faster than is normal for your race by 10 feet. This benefit applies only when you are wearing no armor, light armor, or medium armor, and not carrying a heavy load." special="Apply this bonus before modifying your speed due to any armor worn or load carried. This bonus stacks with any other bonuses to your land speed."}

::ab[Uncanny Dodge (Ex)]{jl=strip l=2 icon=def flavor="You can react to danger before your senses would normally allow you to do so." passive="You cannot be caught ‹rule/flat-footed›, nor do you lose your Dexterity bonus to AC if the attacker is invisible. If you already possesses uncanny dodge from a different class, you automatically gain *improved uncanny dodge* (see below) instead." special="You still lose your Dexterity bonus to AC if immobilized, or if an opponent successfully uses the ‹rule/feint› action against you."}

::ab[Blood Sanctuary (Su)]{jl=strip l=3 icon=def flavor="Due to the power of your blood, you can stand confidently amid the effects of spells cast by yourself or your allies." passive="You gain a +2 bonus on saving throws against spells that you or an ally casts."}

::ab[Spells (Basics)]{jl=Spells l=4 icon=magic ability="You gain the ability to cast a small number of arcane spells drawn from the ‹main/bloodrager spell« list›. You do not need to prepare these spells in advance; you can cast any spell you know at any time, assuming you haven't yet used up your allotment of spells per day for the spell's level. The DC for a saving throw against spells you cast is 10 + the spell level + your Constitution modifier." usage="To learn, prepare, or cast a spell, you must have a Charisma score equal to at least 10 + the spell level."}

The bloodrager's selection of spells is limited. At 4th level, a bloodrager knows two 1st-level spells of his choice. A bloodrager gains more spells as he increases in level. Unlike spells per day, the number of spells a bloodrager knows is not affected by his Charisma score, but it is affected by any bonus spells he gains from his bloodline. At 8th level and every 3 levels thereafter, a bloodrager can choose to learn a new spell in place of one he already knows. This swap follows all the same rules as for a sorcerer.

::ab[Blood Casting (Su)]{l=4 icon=magic ability="You gain the ability to cast spells even while bloodraging. You can also cast these spells ‹rule/cast_>defensively› and can make ‹rule/concentration› checks for these spells while bloodraging." special="While bloodraging, you can cast and concentrate on only your bloodrager spells; spells from other classes cannot be cast during this state."}
::ab[Eschew Materials]{l=4 icon=power passive="You gain ‹feat/Eschew Materials› as a bonus feat."}

::ab[Improved Uncanny Dodge (Ex)]{jl=strip icon=def passive="You can no longer be ‹rule/flanked›. This defense denies rogues (or other classes) the ability to ‹ability/sneak attack› you by flanking, unless the attacker has at least four more rogue levels (or levels in the class granting sneak attack) than your bloodrager levels." special="If you already have uncanny dodge from another class, the levels from the classes that grant uncanny dodge stack when determining the minimum rogue level required to flank you."}

::ab[Damage Reduction (Ex)]{jl=strip icon=def l=7 passive="You gain ‹rule/damage reduction› DR 1/-. Subtract 1 from the damage you take each time you're dealt damage from a weapon or a natural attack. Damage reduction can reduce damage to 0, but not below 0." imp10="Your DR becomes 2/-." imp13="Your DR becomes 3/-." imp16="Your DR becomes 4/-." imp19="Your DR becomes 5/-."}

::ab[Greater Bloodrage (Su)]{jl="Other Abilities" icon=boost-def-magic l=11 ability="When you enter a bloodrage, the morale bonus to Strength and Constitution increases to +6 and the morale bonus on Will saves increases to +3. In addition, upon entering a bloodrage, you can apply the effects a bloodrager spell you know of 2nd level or lower to yourself. The spell must have a range of touch or personal. If the spell's duration is greater than 1 round, it instead lasts for the duration of the bloodrage. This use consumes a bloodrager spell slot, as if you had cast the spell; you must have the spell slot available to take advantage of this effect." special="*Greater bloodrage* counts as the barbarian's *greater rage* ability for the purposes of feat prerequisites, feat abilities, magic item abilities, and spell effects."}

::ab[Indomitable Will (Ex)]{l=14 icon=def passive="You gain a +4 bonus on Will saves to resist enchantment spells while bloodraging. This bonus stacks with all other modifiers, including the morale bonus on Will saves you also receives during your bloodrage."}
::ab[Tireless Bloodrage (Su)]{l=17 icon=boost passive="You no longer become fatigued at the end of your bloodrage."}

::ab[Mighty Bloodrage (Su)]{l=20 icon=boost-def-magic ability="When you enter a bloodrage, the morale bonus to Strength and Constitution increases to +8, and the morale bonus on Will saves increases to +4. Furthermore, the spell you can apply to yourself at the beginning of a bloodrage due to the *greater bloodrage* class feature is not limited to only spells of 2nd level or lower."}
::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›

When a character reaches the 20th level of a class, she gains a powerful class feature or ability, sometimes referred to as a capstone. The following section provides new capstones for characters to select at 20th level. A character can select one of the following capstones in place of the capstone provided by her class. Some capstones are for specific classes, while others are for a range of classes that qualify for them. In some cases, a capstone specifies what ability it replaces. A character can't select a new capstone if she has previously traded away her class capstone via an archetype. Clerics and wizards can receive a capstone at 20th level, despite not having one to begin with.
| Capstone | Benefit |

| Deep Magics (Su) | At 20th level, the character's repertoire of spells deepens dramatically. She gains an additional spell known for each spell level she can cast. A character of any class with spells known can select this capstone. |
| Empowered Bloodrage (Su) | At 20th level, the bloodrager is as much spell as mortal flesh. When the bloodrager enters a bloodrage, he can apply two spells to himself rather than one. |

| Perfect Body, Flawless Mind (Ex) | At 20th level, the character's endless training and study has resulted in an unmatched mastery of the self. The character increases her ability scores by a collective total of 8. For example, she can increase one score by 8, or one score by 5 and another by 3, or four scores by 2, and so on. Characters of any class can select this ability. |

| The Boss (Ex) | At 20th level, the character has become more than just a lone hero - she has become one of the senior figures of her field, with powers and responsibilities to match. The character becomes one of the leading figures in some manner of group or organization, as appropriate to the campaign and the setting. A wizard might become the dean of an arcane university or mages' guild, a fighter could command a mercenary army or a city guard, a cleric might lead a major temple or her own sect, and so forth. The player and the GM should work together to determine the specifics. The character gains the ‹feat/Leadership› feat if she does not already have it, and the number of followers that the feat grants is multiplied by 10 (although depending on the campaign and setting, the position may grant other powers as well). If multiple characters in a party select this capstone, the GM may consider pooling them to grant the players a particularly large and powerful organization, such as a small kingdom. Characters of any class can select this ability. |

| With This Sword (Ex) | At 20th level, the character's blade has become as well-known as the character herself. The character selects one item she has - preferably something iconic and significant, such as a weapon or arcane bond. The item becomes a ‹rule/minor »artifact<s› and gains 100,000 gp worth of new powers. The player and the GM should work together to select the new powers, with an eye towards making something memorable yet campaign-appropriate. Characters of any class can select this ability. |

| Won't Stay Dead (Ex) | At 20th level, the character becomes a paragon of resilience. Once per week, if the character is killed, petrified, or otherwise removed from play, the character manages to survive by some dint of skill or luck and returns at the end of the combat or the scene (GM's discretion). The player and the GM should work together to ensure that the method of the character's survival is at least vaguely plausible, if unlikely. Characters of any class can select this ability. |
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::blockquote{className="hanging striped"}

**Catfolk:** Add 1 foot to the bloodrager's base speed. In combat, this has an effect only for every five increases in base speed. This bonus stacks with the bloodrager's fast movement class feature, and applies under the same conditions as that feature.[^A]
**Drow:** Add a +1/2 bonus to ‹rule/concentration› checks to cast or maintain spells when taking damage.[^B]
**Dwarf:** Increase the bloodrager's total number of bloodrage rounds per day by 1.[^C]

**Elf:** Add 1 foot to the bloodrager's base speed. In combat, this only has an effect for every five increases in base speed. This bonus stacks with the bloodrager's fast movement class feature, and applies under the same conditions and that feature.[^C]
**Gathlain:** Add 1 additional round of bloodrage per day.[^D]
**Gnome:** Add 1/4 to the bloodrager's effective class level when determining the power of her bloodrager bloodline powers.[^E]
**Half-elf:** Increase the bonus gained from blood sanctuary by 1/4.[^E]
**Halfling:** Gain a +1/4 dodge bonus to AC while bloodraging against creatures at least one size category larger than the bloodrager.[^E]
**Half-orc:** Increase the bloodrager's total number of bloodrage rounds per day by 1.[^F]
**Human:** Increase the bloodrager's total number of bloodrage rounds per day by 1.[^F]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^G]
**Shabti:** Add 1/4 to the bloodrager's effective class level when determining the power of her bloodrager bloodline powers.[^H]
**Strix:** Increase the bloodrager's total number of bloodrage rounds per day by 1.[^I]
**Wyvaran:** Add 1 to the bloodrager's total rounds of bloodrage each day.[^J]
::h3[Archetypes]{jl}

::archetype[Ancestral Harbinger]{c=bloodrager r="Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; 6th, 12th, and 18th-level Bloodline Feats" e="While all bloodragers tap into their bloodlines to fuel the arcane energies they use in battle, some have the ability to call directly to the spirits of their ancestors to aid them, summoning spirits to fight for them or inspire their allies."}

::archetype[Blood Conduit]{c=bloodrager r="Fast Movement; Bloodline Feats; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Indomitable Will" e="Blood conduits learn to channel their arcane might directly through their flesh, without the need for mystical words or gestures."}

::archetype[Bloodline Familiars]{c=bloodrager r="Bloodline; Bloodline Spells; 1st-Level Bloodline Power" e="Those with an inherent connection to magic often attract creatures who feel a similar instinctive pull toward magical forces."}

::archetype[Bloodrider]{c=bloodrager r="Fast Movement; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; 9th-level Bloodline Feat" e="In the world's wild lands, a mount is an advantage in both everyday life and the dealing of death"}

::archetype[Bloody-Knuckled Rowdy]{c=bloodrager r="Damage Reduction; Spellcasting; Fast Movement; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="The bloody-knuckled rowdy focuses on tricks and maneuvers that are quick and effective, duplicating combat styles from professional brawlers, street thugs, and even trapped animals."}

::archetype[Crossblooded Rager]{c=bloodrager r="Bonus Feats; Bonus Spells; Bloodline Powers; Saving Throws" e="While most bloodragers manifest only one bloodline, there are some that, through some quirk of heredity or the conjunction of other powers, manifest two."}

::archetype[Enlightened Bloodrager]{c=bloodrager r="Spellcasting; 1st, 4th-level Bloodline Power; Bloodrage; Bonus Spells; 7th, 13th, 19th-level Damage Reduction" e="Troubled by overwhelming rage and mystic forces beyond their comprehension, bloodragers have been traveling to the Magaambya for generations, seeking the peace and enlightenment promised by students of Old-Mage Jatembe."}

::archetype[Greenrager]{c=bloodrager r="Blood Sanctuary; 6th and 9th-level Bloodline Feats" e="Typically, nature finds its greatest harmony with divine magic, but sometimes a connection with the natural world manifest itself through the arcane current in the veins of the bloodragers called greenragers."}

::archetype[Hag-Riven]{c=bloodrager r="Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Fast Movement; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Damage Reduction" e="All changelings have the potential to transform into hags, but the process is a slow one, requiring several nights to complete. When left incomplete - by virtue of a wily changeling's escape or her mother's death - the creature is scarred and saturated in arcane power."}

::archetype[Id Rager]{c=bloodrager r="Bloodline; Bloodline Spells; Bloodline Powers; Spellcasting; Eschew Materials; Bloodline Feats" e="An id rager lacks a supernatural taint to his blood, instead drawing power from pure emotion."}

::archetype[Metamagic Rager]{c=bloodrager r="Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="While metamagic is difficult for many bloodragers to utilize, a talented few are able to channel their bloodrage in ways that push their spells to impressive ends."}

::archetype[Primalist]{c=bloodrager r="Bloodline" e="While bloodrage powers come from the very essence of a bloodrager's being and are often strict and immutable, some bloodragers tap into ancient traditions and primitive wisdom to enhance their rages with something more primal."}

::archetype[Prowler at World's End]{c=bloodrager r="Bloodline Powers; 9th, 12th, 15th-level Bloodline Feats; Greater Bloodrage; Mighty Bloodrage" e="Many catfolk bloodragers capable of communing with the ancient spirits of creation take on the burden of protecting the world from the sinister forces that exist at the world's fringes."}

::archetype[Rageshaper]{c=bloodrager r="Blood Sanctuary; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="All bloodragers blend the unpredictable surge of arcane power with the savage fury of battle lust. For most, their rage is a conduit for the eldritch power locked in their heritage, but for a rageshaper, the latent magical energies in his blood bring about physical transformations and facilitate the blending of arcana and aggression into a deadly synthesis that few other barbarians (or even other bloodragers) can match."}

::archetype[Spelleater]{c=bloodrager r="Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Damage Reduction" e="Where other bloodragers learn to avoid or shrug off minor damage of all sorts, spelleaters tap into the power of their bloodline in order to heal damage as it comes, and can even cannibalize their own magical energy to heal more damage and continue taking the fight to the enemy."}

::archetype[Steelblood]{c=bloodrager r="Armor Proficiency; Fast Movement; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Damage Reduction" e="Most bloodragers prefer light armor, but some learn the secret of using heavy armors."}

::archetype[Symbol Striker]{c=bloodrager r="Class Skills; Fast Movement; 6th, 9th, 12th, 18th-level Bloodline Feats" e="Symbol strikers are unique form of rune-wielding berserker spellcasters whose tradition originates from Dongun Hold, a Sky Citadel in Alkenstar"}

::archetype[Untouchable Rager]{c=bloodrager r="Spells; Blood Casting; Eschew Materials; Bloodline Spells" e="While most bloodragers are known for their inexplicable ability to focus their bloodline into a horrifying mix of martial terror and spellcasting fury, from time to time a bloodrager's bloodline acts differently."}

::archetype[Urban Bloodrager]{c=bloodrager r="Class Skills; Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Bloodrage; Blood Sanctuary; Damage Reduction" e="Like the urban barbarian, the urban bloodrager has learned to control her rage in so-called polite society."}
